message: This doesn't work for me. No output at all

replies.message:
Hey Jacob,

You should follow the guide online @ the Github page to make sure you have everything you need installed. You can also try an older version and see if this helps, as the newer one is a "beta" version.

If you need more help, provide more information here or on Github and I would love to help you out. :)

message:
No output on the GUI version, with both prerequisites installed. Just says "Successfully Unlinked!"

Any help would be appreciated!
No output, already followed all the steps from Github.

Hi, Thanks for the application, but as others say, it does not work for me either.
The application just says inmediately "The unlinking is complete!" after you press the "Unlink" button.
EDIT: Just for future readers. This one does not work, but I managed to make the original (not GUI fork) one to work. Download this package:
https://github.com/gnoling/...
Just press the "Code" > "Download zip" button.
Open the zip, extract the dist/unlinkmkv-win64.7z archive. This is a package the original author setup as a portable windows release, which just works. Extract oit on a Windows machine (win10 in my case). Then add the extracted zip filder to your PATH variable (in my case "Z:\Tools\unlinkmkv").
Copy your set of linked mkv files to same directory.
Open a CMD, go to your path and just write "unlinkmkv". It will do the job.

Greetings.
Correctly setted up and everything work except for the output itself. FIles are unlinked but no OP or EDs
